name: Add Binary
description: Given two binary strings a and b, return their sum as a binary string.
constraints: 1 <= a.length, b.length <= 10^4. a and b consist only of '0' or '1'. Each string does not contain leading zeros except '0' itself.

name: All Divisors of a Number
description: Given an integer n, find all the divisors of n and return them in sorted order. A divisor of n is any integer d such that n % d == 0.

name: Check if ith Bit is Set
description: Given a number n and a bit position i (0-indexed from right), check whether the ith bit of n is set (1) or not (0).

name: Minimum Bit Flips to Convert
description: Given two integers start and goal, return the minimum number of bit flips to convert start to goal. A bit flip is changing a bit from 0 to 1 or from 1 to 0. Hint: count set bits in start XOR goal.

name: Counting Bits
description: Given an integer n, return an array ans of length n + 1 such that for each i (0 <= i <= n), ans[i] is the number of 1's in the binary representation of i.
python_template:

name: Divide Two Integers
description: Given two integers dividend and divisor, divide them without using multiplication, division, or mod operators. Return the quotient truncated toward zero. If the quotient overflows 32-bit signed integer range, return 2^31 - 1.

name: Missing Number
description: Given an array nums containing n distinct numbers in the range [0, n], return the only number in the range that is missing from the array. Solve in O(n) time and O(1) extra space using XOR or math.
constraints: n == nums.length. 1 <= n <= 10^4. 0 <= nums[i] <= n. All values are unique.

name: Number of 1 Bits
description: Given a positive integer n, return the number of set bits (1s) in its binary representation (also known as the Hamming weight).

name: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of two. Otherwise, return false. An integer is a power of two if there exists x such that n == 2^x. Hint: a power of two has exactly one set bit.

name: Prime Factorisation
description: Given a number n, find all its prime factors and return them in sorted order. Each prime factor should appear as many times as it divides n.
python_template:

name: Reverse Bits
description: Reverse bits of a given 32-bit unsigned integer. Return the integer formed by reversing all 32 bits.
constraints: The input is a 32-bit unsigned integer.

name: Reverse Integer
description: Given a signed 32-bit integer x, return x with its digits reversed. If reversing x causes the value to go outside the signed 32-bit integer range [-2^31, 2^31 - 1], then return 0.

name: Set the Rightmost Unset Bit
description: Given a non-negative integer n, set the rightmost unset (0) bit of n to 1 and return the result. If all bits are already set, return n as-is.

name: Sieve of Eratosthenes
description: Given an integer n, return all prime numbers less than or equal to n using the Sieve of Eratosthenes algorithm.

name: XOR from L to R
description: Given two integers L and R, find the XOR of all numbers from L to R (inclusive). Use the pattern: XOR(1 to N) cycles every 4 values. So XOR(L to R) = XOR(1 to R) ^ XOR(1 to L-1).
